channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Police Investigate Discovery of Mass Grave in Kericho Containing 14 Bodies
item.description: The Kenyan police have launched an investigation after the discovery of what is suspected to be a mass grave containing 14 mutilated bodies in a public cemetery in Kericho. The incident was reported b...

item.title: BIR grants VAT exemption for indigenous natural gas deals
item.description: The Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) has officially exempted transactions involving indigenous natural gas from value-added tax (VAT). This measure is expected to support the local energy sector and r...

item.title: Live Updates: Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Performance on March 23, 2026
item.description: The Wall Street Journal's live coverage tracks real-time movements in major U.S. stock indices on March 23, 2026, focusing on the Dow Jones Industrial Average, S&P 500, and Nasdaq Composite. As market...

item.title: Government Restricts High-Octane Fuel Use Amidst Economic Concerns
item.description: In a move aimed at conserving resources and managing the country’s economic situation, Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif has issued a directive banning the use of high-octane fuel in all government vehicl...

item.title: Orlando Pirates rising star Moses Mphekgoane earns finance degree while building football career
item.description: At just 21 years old, Orlando Pirates DStv Diski Challenge defender Moses Mphekgoane has balanced football and academics with outstanding success. Nicknamed the 'Scholar-Warrior', he recently complete...

item.title: How supporting women’s leadership and green initiatives is helping Tanzania become more sustainable
item.description: The article explains how putting women at the centre of development plans is helping Tanzania to become more sustainable and equal. It shows that women and girls are critical in areas such as educatio...

item.title: HYPREP Strengthens Efforts to Provide Clean and Safe Water Across Ogoni Communities
item.description: The Hydrocarbon Pollution Remediation Project (HYPREP) don use World Water Day remind people say dem still dey serious about providing clean water for Ogoni land, according to the United Nations Envir...

item.title: Kenya Launches $38bn Infrastructure Fund Amid Concerns of Political Influence and Corruption
item.description: Kenya has introduced a bold National Infrastructure Fund aimed at financing KSh5 trillion ($38 billion) in infrastructure projects over the next decade. Spearheaded by President William Ruto, the init...

item.title: The Ongoing US-Iran Conflict: A Modern Clash Rooted in Ancient Rivalries
item.description: The article reflects on the escalating US-Iran conflict, comparing it to ancient Persian warfare tactics, specifically drawing parallels with the Persian Empire, which existed 600 years before Christ....

item.title: Pakistan Armed Forces Reaffirm Commitment to National Security and Sovereignty on Pakistan Day
item.description: On the occasion of Pakistan Day 2026, the Armed Forces of Pakistan reaffirmed their steadfast commitment to defending the nation’s sovereignty, ensuring internal stability, and eradicating terrorism. ...

item.title: Iran Employs Insurgency to Pressure US and Prolong Conflict
item.description: This article examines Iran’s strategy in its current conflict with the United States, arguing that direct military victory is unlikely, and Iran is instead focusing on a long-term insurgency to exhaus...

item.title: Delta Government Recognises Autistic Teen for Long-Distance Cycling Awareness Campaign
item.description: On Sunday for Asaba, Delta State government welcome 16-year-old autistic cyclist, Kanyeyachukwu Tagbo-Okeke, as he dey do im endurance ride from Enugu go Lagos. Kanyeyachukwu waka with him parents, me...

item.title: Trump warns Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz amid rising energy crisis and escalating Middle East conflict
item.description: The Daily Mail reports that tensions in the Middle East have reached a critical point as former US President Donald Trump issued a 48-hour ultimatum for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a key mari...

item.title: BDC’s Support Helps NP Aerospace Expand in Growing Defence Sector
item.description: NP Aerospace, a London, Ont.-based company, has been able to scale up its operations and take on new opportunities, thanks in part to support from the Business Development Bank of Canada (BDC). James ...

item.title: Police Arrest Six Suspects in Kisumu and Trans Nzoia, Recover Stolen Goods
item.description: Kenya police have successfully arrested six suspects in separate operations in Kisumu and Trans Nzoia counties as part of a broader effort to curb organised crime and theft. In Kisumu, four individual...

item.title: Two killed in tragic runway collision at New York's LaGuardia airport
item.description: A horrific accident took place at New York’s LaGuardia airport when an Air Canada Express plane, arriving from Montreal, collided with a fire truck on the runway. The crash, which occurred late Sunday...

item.title: Barry Keoghan opens up about dealing with online bullying and self-image struggles
item.description: Irish actor Barry Keoghan recently opened up about the emotional toll of online criticism regarding his appearance. Known for his standout performances in Hollywood films, Keoghan shared that negativi...

item.title: New Study Reveals Why Acne Scars Are Hard to Treat and How to Address Them Effectively
item.description: Acne scars often persist despite using various treatments like creams, peels, or lasers. A groundbreaking study conducted by doctors at ABVIMS and Ram Manohar Lohia Hospital in New Delhi reveals that ...

item.title: Kajiado County Officials Summoned Over 17-Year-Old Sh1.2 Billion Debt
item.description: Kajiado County is facing serious legal trouble over a Sh1.2 billion fine owed to Kilimanjaro Safari Club since 2009. The High Court in Nairobi, through the Judicial Review division, has issued a show-...

item.title: Floods Destroy Section of Kitale-Morpus Road, Authorities Reroute Traffic
item.description: Heavy rains over the weekend in Kenya’s Rift Valley region have caused serious damage to part of the Kitale–Morpus road, cutting off transport between key towns and inconveniencing motorists heading t...
